text:
I see, there's a <https://savannah.gnu.org/bugs/?65104|new check> in `msgmerge` requiring the reference file to be either ASCII or UTF-8-encoded. Right now the workaround is to move the driver into a shell script, _encoding the `*.po` file into UTF-8 in the process_:

`driver = force-outside-strings %A %B`

```#!/bin/sh
base="$1"
merge="$2"
base_u8=`mktemp`
msgconv -t UTF-8 -o "$base_u8" "$base"
msguniq "$merge" | msgmerge -o "$base" - "$base_u8"
exit=$?
rm -f "$base_u8"
exit $exit```
The script can be made smarter to determine the original encoding and `msgconv` the file back into it. (And also preserve the text width if desired.)

Since merging instead of rebasing should lower the number of conflicts, it will make it possible switch to `po3way` and preserve the R-side fixes such as <https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/commit/05b5978a7bfa3280ddfd23baa4030add1a2e0930|r89992>, which currently have to be discarded.

*Edit*: a smarter script:
```#!/bin/sh
base="$1"
merge="$2"

before=`sha1sum "$base"`

# msgmerge now requires the merge base to be UTF-8 if not ASCII
base_charset=`sed -n '/^"Content-Type: /{s/^"Content-Type: .*charset=\(.*\)\\\\n"$/\1/p;q}' "$merge"`
base_u8=`mktemp`
if [ ! -n "$base_charset" ]; then
	echo "Failed to determine charset in $merge"
	base_charset="UTF-8"
fi
msgconv -t UTF-8 -o "$base_u8" "$base"
# assuming UTF-8 locale
max_width=`wc -L "$base_u8" | sed 's/ .*//'`

# override matching translations in the base file from the merged-in file
msguniq "$merge" | msgmerge - "$base_u8" | msgconv -t $base_charset -w $max_width -o "$base"

after=`sha1sum "$base"`

if [ "$before" = "$after" ]; then
	echo "file unchanged after msgmerge!"
fi

rm -f "$base_u8"```
Errors are deliberately ignored because they all correspond to historical damaged files (such as a few Latin-1 strings in a `*.po` marked as `charset=UTF-8`) where a following commit fixes the problem anyway.

merge="$2"
base_u8=`mktemp`
msgconv -t UTF-8 -o "$base_u8" "$base"
msguniq "$merge" | msgmerge -o "$base" - "$base_u8"

exit $exit

the file back into it. (And also preserve the text width if desired.)

Since merging instead of rebasing should lower the number of conflicts, it will make it possible switch to
and preserve the R-side fixes such as
, which currently have to be discarded.

#!/bin/sh
base="$1"
merge="$2"

before=`sha1sum "$base"`

# msgmerge now requires the merge base to be UTF-8 if not ASCII
base_charset=`sed -n '/^"Content-Type: /{s/^"Content-Type: .*charset=\(.*\)\\\\n"$/\1/p;q}' "$merge"`
base_u8=`mktemp`
if [ ! -n "$base_charset" ]; then
	echo "Failed to determine charset in $merge"
	base_charset="UTF-8"
fi
msgconv -t UTF-8 -o "$base_u8" "$base"
# assuming UTF-8 locale
max_width=`wc -L "$base_u8" | sed 's/ .*//'`

# override matching translations in the base file from the merged-in file
msguniq "$merge" | msgmerge - "$base_u8" | msgconv -t $base_charset -w $max_width -o "$base"

after=`sha1sum "$base"`

if [ "$before" = "$after" ]; then
	echo "file unchanged after msgmerge!"
fi

rm -f "$base_u8"
